meta.name: French Country Ratatouille
meta.subtitle: Provençal-style summer vegetable stew (rustic, not the spiral confit).

source.name: ssa.cuisines.french_country
notes: Canonical SSA recipe: rustic stovetop ratatouille with step timers and checks.
Designed to be batch-friendly, leftovers-friendly, and freezer-friendly.

summary: A classic rustic ratatouille: eggplant, zucchini, peppers, and tomatoes simmered with olive oil, garlic, and herbs until silky and aromatic. Great hot, warm, or cold.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a side; 3–4 as a main with bread, eggs, or grains.

notes: Flavor improves after resting 15–30 minutes.
If vegetables are very watery, simmer uncovered longer to concentrate.
Bright tomato + olive oil richness with gentle garlic/herb backbone.
Optional anchovy adds savory depth without tasting fishy.

optional.label: Sheet pan
optional.notes: Optional: roast eggplant/zucchini first for deeper flavor and less watery stew.

notes: If using anchovy (optional), contains fish.
If using Parmesan garnish (optional), contains dairy.

ingredients.label: Eggplant, cut into 3/4-inch cubes
notes: If eggplant is very large/seedy, peel alternating strips and discard spongy seed core.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Used in stages; adjust at end depending on tomato acidity and salt level.

ingredients.label: Yellow summer squash, cut into 3/4-inch half-moons
notes: Optional; replace with another zucchini if unavailable.

ingredients.label: Yellow or orange bell pepper, diced
Olive oil
notes: Ratatouille is an olive-oil dish; don't be shy, but adjust to preference.

ingredients.label: Ripe tomatoes, diced (or 1 can crushed tomatoes)
notes: If using canned: 1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes works well.
If tomatoes are bland, add a pinch of sugar or extra herbs.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
notes: Optional but helpful for deeper tomato flavor if tomatoes are watery.

ingredients.label: Fresh thyme leaves (or dried thyme)
notes: If fresh sprigs: use 4–6 sprigs and remove stems later.

ingredients.label: Herbes de Provence
notes: Optional; use instead of (or in addition to) thyme for a classic Provençal profile.

ingredients.label: Red wine vinegar (or balsamic) for finishing
notes: Optional: add only if the stew tastes flat; tomatoes may provide enough acidity.

ingredients.label: Anchovy fillet, minced (optional umami booster)
notes: Melts into the dish and adds depth; omit for vegan/vegetarian.

steps.title: Optional: salt eggplant to reduce bitterness and moisture
instructions: Toss eggplant cubes with 1 tsp of the salt and let sit 20 minutes in a colander.
Pat dry with towels to remove released moisture.
checks: Eggplant looks slightly collapsed and has released moisture.
Eggplant is patted reasonably dry before sautéing.

steps.title: Sauté eggplant until lightly browned
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a wide pan or Dutch oven over medium-high.
Add eggplant in a single layer if possible (work in batches if needed).
Cook 6–8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned and starting to soften.
Transfer eggplant to a bowl.
checks: Eggplant has some golden-brown edges (not pale/steamed).
Eggplant is partially softened but not fully collapsed.

steps.title: Sauté zucchini/squash to keep texture
instructions: Add 1 tbsp olive oil to the pan.
Sauté zucchini (and optional yellow squash) for 4–6 minutes until just starting to brown.
Transfer to the bowl with eggplant.
checks: Zucchini is lightly browned but still holds shape.

steps.title: Cook onion and peppers until sweet and softened
instructions: Add remaining 1 tbsp olive oil to the pan.
Add onion with a pinch of salt; cook 3 minutes.
Add diced peppers and cook 6–8 minutes until softened and glossy.
If using anchovy, add it now and stir 30 seconds until it dissolves.

steps.title: Bloom garlic and concentrate tomato base
instructions: Add garlic and stir 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes (or crushed tomatoes).
If using tomato paste, stir it in now.
Add thyme, optional herbes de Provence, and optional bay leaf.

steps.title: Simmer to meld flavors and reach desired texture
instructions: Return eggplant and zucchini/squash to the pot.
Stir to combine and bring to a gentle simmer.
Partially cover and simmer 25–40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are tender and sauce is glossy.
If it gets watery, simmer uncovered for the last 10 minutes.
checks: Vegetables are tender but not mushy (unless you prefer very soft).
Sauce looks glossy and slightly reduced.

steps.title: Season and finish with herbs
instructions: Remove bay leaf and any woody herb stems (if used).
Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
If the flavor tastes flat, add 1 tsp red wine vinegar (optional).
Fold in basil and/or parsley just before serving.
checks: Seasoning is balanced (tomato acidity supported by salt).
Fresh herbs smell bright and not cooked out.

steps.title: Rest briefly and serve
instructions: Let ratatouille rest 10–15 minutes (flavors deepen as it cools slightly).
Serve warm or at room temperature with crusty bread, over rice, with eggs, or as a side to roast chicken or fish.
Optional: top with Parmesan if not vegan.
checks: Dish has thickened slightly after resting.

notes: Excellent alongside roast chicken or served with a fried egg and bread for a simple meal.
Leftovers are great as a pasta sauce base.
Cool and store airtight. Flavors improve by day 2.
Serve cold as a salad-like side or reheat gently.
Freeze in portions. Texture softens after freezing (still delicious).
Use thawed ratatouille in soups, shakshuka-style eggs, or pasta sauce.
Reheat gently over low heat; add a drizzle of olive oil if it tastes dull.

core: Cook vegetables to a gentle simmer; avoid scorching by stirring occasionally.
Cool leftovers promptly and refrigerate within 2 hours.

notes: Rustic ratatouille is intentionally soft and saucy—if you want firmer vegetables, reduce simmer time and sauté vegetables a bit more aggressively.
If tomatoes are out of season, canned crushed tomatoes often taste better than bland fresh ones.
Optional shortcut: roast eggplant and zucchini at 425°F for ~20 minutes (tossed with oil/salt), then simmer with the tomato-pepper base.
Roast eggplant and zucchini at 425°F for 18–22 minutes, then simmer in tomato base 15–20 minutes.
Use garlic-infused oil and reduce onion, or replace onion with the green tops of scallions (if tolerated).
Serve with eggs (poached or fried), chickpeas stirred in, or alongside sausage/roast chicken.

remixIdeas: Ratatouille shakshuka (simmer and crack in eggs)
Pasta sauce base (reduce slightly, add basil and Parmesan)
